display.purpose: Hero/marketing contexts only; not used inside the review session.

card-prompt.purpose: The flashcard's question/answer text, capped at 46ch — read aloud as much as read.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrow tags (FRONT, YOUR ANSWER, VOICE MODE) and field labels.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Small floating elements: tooltips, small popovers.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Standard floating-above-the-page shadow: modals, the teaching bottom sheet.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Maximum lift for the most prominent overlays.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for hover/state transitions.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Reserved; rarely used given the calm-motion doctrine.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Signal Cyan fill, the default call-to-action.
components.css: .ds-btn{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;gap:8px;font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',system-ui,sans-serif;font-weight:600;border-radius:12px;border:0.5px solid transparent;cursor:pointer;height:42px;padding:0 18px;font-size:15px;transition:background .16s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1),box-shadow .16s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1),transform .12s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1);} .ds-btn:focus-visible{outline:none;box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(32,208,255,.18);} .ds-btn:active{transform:translateY(1px);} .ds-btn-primary{background:#20d0ff;color:#04222e;} .ds-btn-primary:hover{background:#58dcff;} .ds-btn-primary:active{background:#11b6e6;}

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Raised-surface fill for the second-priority action.

components.name: Flashcard
components.description: The signature flashcard surface — flat, bordered, generous padding, centered prompt text.
components.html:
FrontSpanish Verbs

What is the past participle of hacer?

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Labelled text field with hint/error states and the cyan focus glow.
components.html: Saved to localStorage only.
components.css: .ds-field{display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:8px;font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',system-ui,sans-serif;} .ds-field__label{font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.09em;color:#6c8097;} .ds-input{width:100%;font-family:inherit;font-size:15px;color:#eaf1f8;background:#19293f;border:0.5px solid rgba(255,255,255,.08);border-radius:8px;padding:11px 14px;outline:none;transition:border-color .16s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1),box-shadow .16s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1);} .ds-input::placeholder{color:#6c8097;} .ds-input:focus{border-color:#20d0ff;box-shadow:0 0 0 3px rgba(32,208,255,.14);} .ds-field__hint{font-size:12px;color:#6c8097;line-height:1.5;}

components.name: Grade Chip
components.description: Again/Hard/Good/Easy grade scale, kept verbatim from Anki.

components.name: Record Button
components.description: The session's primary round control; pulses red while recording, cyan while listening.
components.html: <button class="ds-record" aria-label="Record"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"><path d="M12 1a4 4 0 00-4 4v6a4 4 0 008 0V5a4 4 0 00-4-4z" fill="currentColor"/><path d="M19 11a7 7 0 01-14 0M12 18v4" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-record{width:64px;height:64px;border-radius:999px;background:#20d0ff;color:#04222e;border:none;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;cursor:pointer;box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(32,208,255,.18);animation:ds-record-pulse 1.6s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1) infinite;} .ds-record--active{background:#e74c3c;color:#fff;box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(231,76,60,.18);animation:ds-record-pulse-rec 1.1s cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1) infinite;} @keyframes ds-record-pulse{0%,100%{box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(32,208,255,.18);}50%{box-shadow:0 0 0 8px rgba(32,208,255,.1);}} @keyframes ds-record-pulse-rec{0%,100%{box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(231,76,60,.18);}50%{box-shadow:0 0 0 10px rgba(231,76,60,.1);}} @media (prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.ds-record,.ds-record--active{animation:none;}}

narrative.overview: Anki Convo's interface is a quiet instrument panel glowing in the dark — the surface a reviewer watches while talking, not reading. Deep navy fills nearly the whole screen; everything that isn't navy is either a hairline border holding a surface together or one of exactly two signal colors doing real work: Signal Cyan for the system listening, acting, or focused, and a sparing lime for the moment something is confirmed correct or new. There is no gradient, no texture, no imagery — depth comes from stacked navy surfaces and a 0.5px hairline border, not from decoration. This rejects the gamified, mascot-driven, confetti-and-streak-badge school of language apps, and it rejects the generic cluttered SaaS/edtech dashboard just as firmly — both push noise into space this system reserves for the next spoken turn.
keyCharacteristics: Flat dark navy surfaces, hairline borders, zero imagery or gradient
Exactly two signal colors (cyan = active/focus/voice, lime = correct/new) — everything else is navy, text, or the inherited Anki grade scale
Generous, centered single-column layouts (440px setup, 680px review) — never a dashboard grid

rules.name: The Two-Signal Rule
rules.body: Only cyan and lime carry meaning. If a third accent color appears anywhere outside the inherited Anki grade scale, it's wrong.

rules.name: The No-Gray Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is navy-tinted. A flat, hue-0 gray anywhere in the UI is an error.

rules.name: The Spoken-Width Rule
rules.body: Card prompt text caps at 46ch — wider than the 65–75ch body guideline, because it's read aloud in a single breath.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: A resting card or row with a shadow is a mistake; the border already does that job. Shadow is reserved for things that genuinely float above the layout.

dos: Do keep every neutral — background, surface, text, border — navy-tinted; no hue-0 gray.
Do reserve Signal Cyan for listening/acting/focus and lime for confirmed/positive/new; nothing else gets a third accent color.
Do use the 0.5px hairline border as the default way surfaces separate from the navy field behind them.
Do keep the Again/Hard/Good/Easy grade scale and its exact colors verbatim from Anki.
Do use the cyan glow ring for focus states, never a hard default outline.
Do ease motion out with the exponential ease-out curve and provide a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
donts: Don't add shadows to resting cards, rows, or panels.
Don't introduce a gamified, mascot-driven, Duolingo-style register: no streak badges, no confetti, no cutesy illustration carrying tone.
Don't build a generic, cluttered SaaS/edtech dashboard layout.
Don't use gradients, photography, or texture anywhere.
Don't put lime as body text on a dark surface.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing on any transition.
